title: From Hypotheses to Factors: Constrained LLM Agents in Cryptocurrency Markets
summary: LLM agents are promising tools for empirical discovery, but their flexibility can also turn discovery into uncontrolled search. We study how to use agents under a reproducible protocol through cryptocurrency factor discovery. Our framework casts the task as sequential hypothesis search: an agent reads an append-only experiment trace, proposes falsifiable factor hypotheses, and maps them to executable recipes, while a deterministic engine enforces fixed data splits, selection gates, transaction costs, and portfolio tests. Candidate actions are restricted to a point-in-time factor DSL, making both successful and failed hypotheses auditable. A ridge-combined portfolio trained only on 2020--2022 data achieves a 44.55% annualized return and Sharpe ratio of 1.55 in the 2024--2026 pure out-of-sample period after a 5 basis point one-way trading cost.
authors: Yikuan Huang, Zheqi Fan, Kaiqi Hu, Yifan Ye
reason_zh: 这篇论文提出了一种利用受限LLM代理进行加密货币市场因子发现的创新方法。它强调了可复现的协议、顺序假设搜索、固定数据分割、交易成本和投资组合测试，这些对于因子构造、严谨的回测和策略设计至关重要。摘要中报告的显著的样本外收益和夏普比率（44.55%年化收益，夏普比率1.55）表明了其强大的实践影响力。对于统计学硕士而言，这种结合了前沿AI技术与严格量化研究流程的方法，在因子发现和验证方面具有极高的参考价值。
